content.problemStatement: You are given an integer sequence AAA with length NNN. Find the number of ordered pairs of positive integers (a,b)(a, b)(a,b) such that aaa occurs in AAA at least bbb times and bbb occurs in AAA at least aaa times.
constraints: the sum of N over all test cases does not exceed 3*10^6
content.inputFormat: The first line of the input contains a single integer T denoting the number of test cases. The description of T test cases follows. The first line of each test case contains a single integer N. The second line contains N space-separated integers A1, A2, …, AN.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing one integer — the number of pairs.

sampleTestCases.explanation: For the first test case, there is only one pair (1, 1) that satisfies the condition. For the second test case, there are four pairs (1, 1), (1, 2), (2, 1), and (2, 2) that satisfy the condition. For the third test case, there are three pairs (2, 2), (2, 3), and (3, 2) that satisfy the condition.

solution.approach: To solve this problem, we can use a hashmap to store the frequency of each number in the array. Then, for each number, we can calculate the number of pairs that satisfy the condition. We can do this by iterating over all possible values of b and checking if the frequency of b is greater than or equal to a.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T*N + N^2) — explain: per query is O(N^2), total is O(T*N + T*N^2)
solution.spaceComplexity: O(N) — explain: we need to store the frequency of each number in the hashmap
